{"key_event":"World War I federal mobilization","reasoning":"Income taxation, the Federal Reserve, Progressive regulation, conscription, wartime economic coordination, and speech restrictions substantially increased federal fiscal, administrative, and coercive capacity. Civil society remained strong and became more organized through unions, suffrage, reform, muckraking, and the NAACP, but Jim Crow, labor repression, and wartime censorship limited the net gain.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":7.9,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":8.9}
